content:
Imagine a big robot brain that loves reading stories, books, and everything people have ever written. This robot brain is called a large language model. It learns by looking at tons and tons of words to understand how they fit together. When you ask it a question or start a sentence, it thinks about all the words it has seen before and guesses which word should come next, kind of like playing a giant game of “What comes next?”

Because it has read so much, it can make up new sentences that sound like a real person wrote them. It’s like a magic storyteller inside a computer, using what it learned to help you write a story, answer a question, or even chat. So, when you talk to it, the robot brain is guessing and picking words one by one, creating something new just for you!
